content_en:
112 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

their guide. Allah also sent two pigeons that built a nest and it seemed to the onlooker that they had been there for a long time. All these things were miracles and were more than just resisting the enemy with soldiers.

When the disbelievers stood outside the cave and their talk was heard by the Prophet and his companion, Abu Bakr said, "O Messenger of Allah! If any of them should look under his feet, he would see us.’ The Prophet (peace be upon him) replied, 'O Abu Bakr! What do you think of two (persons) the third of whom is Allah?'"*’ When the Prophet (peace be upon him) saw the sadness of Abu Bakr and his fear for the Messenger and none for himself, he consoled him and said what Allah says,

which means, "Be not sad (or afraid), surely Allah is with us." (At-Tawbah, 9:40)

Abu Bakr accompanied the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) throughout his lifetime and when the Messenger died, he became his successor and used to be called the caliph of the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and when Abu Bakr died, his successors used to be called the Emir of the believers.

They stayed in the cave for three days, then they left it, and continued their journey until Suraqah bin Malik followed them and when he was about to catch them, the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) invoked Allah against him, and his horse stumbled. When he realized that he would never be able to arrest them he offered them money to let him go in peace. He

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 113

offered money to someone who had refused the keys of the treasures of the earth; he offered food to the one who is satisfied. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, “My Lord gives me food and drink during my sleep."* Abu Bakr was the second to embrace Islam, to sacrifice himself, to live an ascetic lifestyle, to accompany him, to take over the caliphate, and they were both of like age, and both he and the prophet (peace be upon him) died due to the effects of poison.

Those who embraced Islam through him were among the ten and they are ‘Uthman, Talhah, Az-Zubair, ‘Abdur-Rahman bin ‘Awf, and Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas. During the time that he embraced Islam, he had forty thousand dirhams, which he spent seeking the pleasure of Allah.

The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "| never benefited from any money as | benefited from the money of Abu Bakr."*’ He is better than the believing man of Fir'awn (Pharaoh), as he hid his Islam while Abu Bakr declared it openly. He is better than the believing man of Al Yasin, as he fought in the cause of Allah for one hour while Abu Bakr did for several years. Although Abu Bakr was a poor man, he loved to spend his money for the sake of Allah, following the verse in which Allah says,

1 eS Cap Seal wall yoo

which means, "Who is he that will lend to Alah a goodly loan." (Al-Baqarah, 2:245)

ét SE MG GY call BY Ga >

114 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

which means, "And A/-Muttaqdn (the pious and righteous - see V.2:2) will be far removed from it (Hell). He who spends his weaith for increase in self-purification." (Al-Lail, 92:17-18)

The Qur'an and history mention his virtues and the Muhajirin (emigrants) and the Ansar (supporters) agreed that he should be the successor of the Prophet (peace be upon him). So you who hated him listen to his merits. Did they not hear what Allah says about him,

which means, "The second of two, when they (Muhammad and Abu Bakr) were in the cave." (At-Tawbah, 9:40)

Abu Bakr answered the Prophet (peace be upon him) when he was Called to islam, and he neither denied nor hesitated. His adherence to Islam never wavered.

e Who was the companion of the Prophet (peace be upon him) during his youth? Who was the first among his companions to embrace Islam? Who was the one who gave legal opinion in his presence and was the fastest to answer? Who was the first to perform prayer with him? Who was the last to lead him in performing prayer? Who was the next after him to die and be laid next to him? The answer to all these questions, is Abu Bakr. He was very understanding and attentive during the days of apostasy and obtained legal opinion from the Qur'an and Sunnah.

e He protected the Messenger with everything at his disposal. He accompanied the Prophet (peace be upon him) during his life and death. How could anyone deny his virtues? To do so would be to try to cover the sun in the middle of the day. He is the best among the Companions, and whoever loves him is a believer.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 115

Oh Rawafid! Our love for him is based on the truth. We followed 'Aly when he said about him, "The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) was content with you in our religion, so why can’t we be content with him in our lives?" By Allah! O ‘Aly, you took our revenge from ArRawéfid. We believe in and acknowledge his favors. Whoever was from Rawéafid, is not from us.

Warning: Great Pearls of Wisdom

Beware of the one who is an enemy to the people of the Qur'an and Sunnah, and do not let them infect you with enmity or you will be the loser.

Beware of two enemies, which were the cause of the doom of many creatures: he who keeps away from Allah and acts on his suspicions, and he who is fascinated by this worldly life and the power he has obtained.

Whoever was granted some kind of special power and readiness, will find his pleasure in using it in this worldly life. Whoever was granted the power to have sexual intercourse, will find his pleasure in utilizing this power, whoever was granted the power to get angry, will! find pleasure in using his anger to get what he wants, whoever was granted the power of eating and drinking, will find pleasure in using his power, whoever was granted the power to gain knowledge, will find pleasure in using this power, and whoever was granted the power to love Allah and turn to Him, he will find his pleasure in using this power to gain closeness to Allah. Pleasures other than those will be less significant and rare.

116 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

Warning: Lessons and Warnings

e O You unarmed, having no good deeds in your account with Allah, beware of the sight of a pious man for he can see your defects from behind a veil. The Prophet (peace be upon him) said, "Beware of the insight of a believer."

5* Recoded by At-Tirmidhy, book of interpretation of Qur'an, no.3127.

* Bil'am bin Ba'dra' wiom Allah revealed the following verse in him, "And recite (O Muhammad SAW) to them the story of him to whom We gave Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.), but he threw them away, so Shaitan (Satan) followed him up, and he became of those who went astray. (Al-A’raf, 7:175)

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 117

which means, "Verily, Allah has purchased of the believers their lives." (At-Tawbah, 9:111)

Allah will never purchase any good (deed) that has not been refined by faith for without it, the good deed cannot enter the world of the pious worshippers of Allah. Hand your good deeds over to the buyer (Allah) before they are ruined through doing bad deeds. In this case the buyer will not accept them. The buyer knew what defects were present in this good deed before buying it, so hand it to Him and be assured that He will never return it.

The value of the good person is estimated according to the buyer (Allah), and the price that will be paid for it (Paradise) is also allotted by Him. If the buyer is Great, then the price is significant, and whatever is sold will be considered precious.

O you who evaluate matters, do you know the value of yourself? The universe was created for your sake.

O you who were fed the milk of piety and changed by the will of Allah, all things are like a tree while you are the fruit; a picture and you are the meaning; a shell and you are the pearl, and buttermilk and you are the butter.

Allah says to His servant, "Whenever you want Me, ask Me to come. Send for Me from yourself and you will find Me near you and do not ask Me to come through another servant, as | am more near to you than the other."

If you knew the true value of yourself, you will never allow yourself to be humiliated by committing sins. Satan

118 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

refused to bow to you when it refused to bow to your father Adam, so how come you reconciled with it and left us? If there is any love in your heart, its effect would appear on your body.
content_id:
112 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Bijak

pemandu mereka. Allah juga mengutus dua ekor merpati yang membangun sarang dan tampak bagi siapa yang melihatnya seolah sarang itu sudah lama ada di sana. Semua ini adalah mukjizat dan lebih dari sekadar menghadapi musuh dengan pasukan.

Ketika orang-orang kafir berdiri di luar gua dan percakapan mereka terdengar oleh Nabi dan sahabatnya, Abu Bakr berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah! Jika salah seorang dari mereka melihat ke bawah kakinya, niscaya ia akan melihat kita." Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) menjawab, "Wahai Abu Bakr! Apa pendapatmu tentang dua orang yang ketiganya adalah Allah?"* Ketika Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) melihat kesedihan Abu Bakr dan rasa takutnya demi Rasulullah, bukan untuk dirinya sendiri, beliau menghiburnya dan mengucapkan apa yang Allah firmankan,

yang bermakna, "Janganlah engkau bersedih (atau takut), sesungguhnya Allah bersama kita." (At-Tawbah, 9:40)

Abu Bakr menemani Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) sepanjang hayat beliau, dan ketika Rasulullah wafat, ia menjadi penggantinya dan biasa dipanggil sebagai khalifah Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam). Ketika Abu Bakr wafat, para penggantinya biasa disebut Amirul Mukminin.

Mereka tinggal di dalam gua selama tiga hari, kemudian keluar dari sana dan melanjutkan perjalanan mereka hingga Suraqah bin Malik mengikuti mereka. Ketika ia hampir dapat menyusul mereka, Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) berdoa kepada Allah untuk menimpakan balasan kepadanya, dan kudanya pun terperosok. Ketika Suraqah menyadari bahwa ia tidak akan pernah mampu menangkap mereka, ia menawarkan harta agar dilepaskan dengan damai. Ia

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Bijak * 113

menawarkan harta kepada seseorang yang telah menolak kunci-kunci perbendaharaan bumi; ia menawarkan makanan kepada orang yang sudah merasa cukup. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda, "Rabb-ku memberiku makan dan minum ketika aku tidur."* Abu Bakr adalah orang kedua yang memeluk Islam, berkorban untuk agama, menjalani hidup zuhud, menemani beliau, mengambil alih khilafah. Keduanya seumuran, dan keduanya pun wafat karena dampak racun.

Mereka yang memeluk Islam melalui perantaraannya termasuk dalam sepuluh orang yang dijamin masuk surga, yaitu 'Utsman, Thalhah, Az-Zubair, 'Abdurrahman bin 'Awf, dan Sa'd bin Abi Waqqas. Pada saat ia memeluk Islam, ia memiliki empat puluh ribu dirham yang ia infakkan demi meraih ridha Allah.

Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda, "Tidak ada harta yang memberiku manfaat sebagaimana harta Abu Bakr."* Ia lebih utama dari lelaki mukmin keluarga Fir'aun (Firaun), sebab lelaki itu menyembunyikan imannya sementara Abu Bakr menampakkannya secara terang-terangan. Ia lebih utama dari lelaki mukmin dari kalangan Aali Yasin, sebab lelaki itu berjuang di jalan Allah hanya selama satu jam sementara Abu Bakr berjuang selama bertahun-tahun. Meskipun Abu Bakr adalah seorang yang sederhana, ia sangat gemar menginfakkan hartanya di jalan Allah, mengamalkan firman Allah,

1 eS Cap Seal wall yoo

yang bermakna, "Siapakah yang mau meminjamkan kepada Allah pinjaman yang baik." (Al-Baqarah, 2:245)

ét SE MG GY call BY Ga >

114 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Bijak

yang bermakna, "Dan orang-orang yang bertaqwa akan dijauhkan darinya (Neraka). (Yaitu) orang yang menginfakkan hartanya untuk menyucikan diri." (Al-Lail, 92:17-18)

Al-Qur'an dan sejarah menyebutkan keutamaannya, dan kaum Muhajirin serta Anshar sepakat bahwa ia yang paling layak menjadi pengganti Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam). Maka dengarkanlah wahai kalian yang membenci beliau, renungkanlah keutamaannya. Tidakkah mereka mendengar apa yang Allah firmankan tentangnya,

yang bermakna, "Yang kedua dari dua orang, ketika keduanya (Muhammad dan Abu Bakr) berada di dalam gua." (At-Tawbah, 9:40)

Abu Bakr menyambut seruan Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) ketika diajak kepada Islam, tanpa menolak maupun ragu-ragu. Kesetiaannya kepada Islam tidak pernah goyah.

e Siapakah sahabat Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) semasa mudanya? Siapakah orang pertama di antara para sahabatnya yang memeluk Islam? Siapakah yang memberikan fatwa di hadapannya dan paling cepat menjawab? Siapakah yang pertama kali shalat bersamanya? Siapakah yang terakhir mengimaminya dalam shalat? Siapakah yang wafat setelahnya dan dikebumikan di sisinya? Jawaban dari semua pertanyaan ini adalah Abu Bakr. Ia sangat memahami dan penuh perhatian di masa riddah, serta mengambil fatwa dari Al-Qur'an dan Sunnah.

e Ia melindungi Rasulullah dengan segala yang dimilikinya. Ia menemani Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) semasa hidup dan matinya. Bagaimana mungkin seseorang mengingkari keutamaannya? Melakukan itu seperti berusaha menutupi matahari di tengah hari. Ia adalah yang terbaik di antara para Sahabat, dan siapa pun yang mencintainya adalah seorang mukmin.

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Bijak * 115

Wahai Rawafid! Kecintaan kami kepadanya didasarkan atas kebenaran. Kami mengikuti 'Ali ketika ia berkata tentangnya, "Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) meridhainya dalam urusan agama kita, maka mengapa kita tidak meridhainya dalam urusan kehidupan kita?" Demi Allah! Wahai 'Ali, engkau telah membalaskan dendamku dari Ar-Rawafid. Kami mengimani dan mengakui keutamaannya. Barang siapa yang termasuk Rawafid, ia bukan bagian dari kami.

Peringatan: Mutiara-Mutiara Hikmah yang Agung

Waspadalah terhadap orang yang memusuhi ahlul Qur'an dan Sunnah, dan janganlah biarkan mereka menularkan permusuhan kepada dirimu, atau engkau akan menjadi yang merugi.

Waspadailah dua musuh yang menjadi penyebab kehancuran banyak makhluk: orang yang menjauh dari Allah dan bertindak berdasarkan prasangkanya, dan orang yang terpesona oleh kehidupan dunia beserta kekuasaan yang telah diperolehnya.

Barang siapa dianugerahi suatu kemampuan dan kecenderungan tertentu, maka ia akan menemukan kesenangannya dalam menggunakannya di kehidupan dunia ini. Barang siapa dianugerahi kemampuan untuk berhubungan suami istri, ia akan menemukan kesenangannya dalam memanfaatkan kemampuan tersebut. Barang siapa dianugerahi kemampuan untuk marah, ia akan menemukan kesenangan dalam menggunakan amarahnya untuk memperoleh apa yang diinginkan. Barang siapa dianugerahi kemampuan makan dan minum, ia akan menemukan kesenangan dalam menggunakan kemampuan itu. Barang siapa dianugerahi kemampuan untuk memperoleh ilmu, ia akan menemukan kesenangan dalam menggunakan kemampuan tersebut. Dan barang siapa dianugerahi kemampuan untuk mencintai Allah dan kembali kepada-Nya, ia akan menemukan kesenangannya dalam menggunakan kemampuan ini untuk mendekatkan diri kepada Allah. Kesenangan-kesenangan selain itu akan terasa lebih kecil nilainya dan jarang ditemukan.

116 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Bijak

Peringatan: Pelajaran dan Nasihat

e Wahai engkau yang tidak bersenjata, yang tidak memiliki amal kebaikan dalam catatan amalmu di sisi Allah, waspadailah pandangan seorang yang bertaqwa karena ia dapat melihat kekuranganmu dari balik tirai. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda, "Waspadailah ketajaman pandangan seorang mukmin."

5* Diriwayatkan oleh At-Tirmidzi, kitab tafsir Al-Qur'an, no.3127.

* Bil'am bin Ba'dra' yang tentangnya Allah menurunkan firman berikut, "Dan bacakanlah (wahai Muhammad SAW) kepada mereka kisah orang yang telah Kami berikan kepadanya ayat-ayat Kami (bukti-bukti, dalil, ayat, pelajaran, tanda-tanda, wahyu, dan sebagainya), namun ia melepaskan diri darinya, sehingga Syaitan mengikutinya dan ia termasuk orang-orang yang sesat." (Al-A'raf, 7:175)

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Kata-Kata Bijak * 117

yang bermakna, "Sesungguhnya Allah telah membeli dari orang-orang mukmin jiwa mereka." (At-Tawbah, 9:111)

Allah tidak akan pernah membeli amal kebaikan apa pun yang tidak disucikan oleh iman, karena tanpa iman amal kebaikan tersebut tidak dapat memasuki alam orang-orang yang bertaqwa dan hamba-hamba Allah yang shalih. Serahkanlah amal kebaikanmu kepada Sang Pembeli (Allah) sebelum rusak karena perbuatan-perbuatan buruk. Dalam keadaan demikian, Sang Pembeli tidak akan menerimanya. Sang Pembeli telah mengetahui cacat apa yang ada pada amal kebaikan itu sebelum membelinya, maka serahkanlah kepada-Nya dan yakinlah bahwa Ia tidak akan pernah mengembalikannya.

Nilai seseorang yang baik dinilai menurut ukuran Sang Pembeli (Allah), dan harga yang akan dibayar untuknya (Surga) juga ditentukan oleh-Nya. Jika Sang Pembeli Maha Agung, maka harganya pun sangat tinggi, dan apa pun yang dijual akan dianggap sangat berharga.

Wahai engkau yang menilai segala perkara, tahukah engkau betapa berharganya dirimu? Alam semesta diciptakan demi dirimu.

Wahai engkau yang telah disusui dengan taqwa dan diubah oleh kehendak Allah, segala sesuatu ibarat pohon sementara engkau adalah buahnya; ibarat gambar sementara engkau adalah maknanya; ibarat cangkang sementara engkau adalah mutiaranya; dan ibarat susu mentega sementara engkau adalah menteganya.

Allah berfirman kepada hamba-Nya, "Kapan pun engkau menginginkan-Ku, mintalah kepada-Ku untuk datang. Panggillah Aku dari dalam dirimu sendiri dan engkau akan menemukan-Ku dekat denganmu. Janganlah engkau meminta-Ku untuk datang melalui perantaraan hamba lain, karena Aku lebih dekat kepadamu daripada siapa pun."

Jika kamu mengetahui nilai dirimu yang sesungguhnya, kamu tidak akan pernah membiarkan dirimu dihinakan dengan melakukan dosa. Iblis

118 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

menolak untuk bersujud kepadamu ketika ia menolak bersujud kepada ayahmu Adam, maka bagaimana bisa kamu berdamai dengannya dan meninggalkan kami? Jika ada cinta dalam hatimu, pengaruhnya akan tampak pada tubuhmu.
